---
title: "Demystifying Linear Regression"
sidebar: false
resources:
  - slides.pdf
format:
  html:
    toc: false
    page-layout: full
---

<div class="project-pdf-slides-root" hidden aria-hidden="true"></div>

::: {.project-pdf-slides-intro}

This presentation was developed for an undergraduate geography course at McMaster University to introduce students to the fundamentals of linear regression modelling. Because the course did not require prior training in statistics, the material was designed to build intuition first - focusing on clear explanations, visual examples, and practical applications relevant to geographic research.

:::

::: {.pdf-slides-embed-bar}
[Open PDF in new tab](slides.pdf){.btn .btn-sm .btn-outline-primary target="_blank" rel="noopener"}
:::

::: {#pdf-slides-frame-wrap .pdf-slides-embed-full}
<iframe src="slides.pdf" title="Transport geography — linear regression lecture slides (PDF)" class="pdf-slides-embed"></iframe>
:::
